flashcards.front: What is the primary goal of unit testing?
flashcards.back: To verify that individual units of code, such as functions or methods, work as expected.
Integration testing is the process of testing multiple units of code together to ensure they interact correctly.
Validation testing is the process of checking that the software meets the requirements and specifications.

flashcards.front: Why is testing important in software development?
flashcards.back: Testing helps identify and fix defects early in the development process, ensuring the software is reliable and performs as expected.
A test case is a set of inputs, expected results, and execution conditions used to test a piece of software.
A test suite is a collection of test cases used to test a piece of software.
Black box testing is a testing technique where the tester has no knowledge of the internal workings of the software.
White box testing is a testing technique where the tester has knowledge of the internal workings of the software.
Gray box testing is a testing technique where the tester has some knowledge of the internal workings of the software.
A testing framework is a set of tools and libraries used to write and run tests.
TDD is a software development process where tests are written before the code.
BDD is a software development process where tests are written in a natural language style.
A mock object is a fake object used in testing to isolate dependencies.
A stub is a small piece of code used in testing to replace a dependency.
A test double is a fake object used in testing to replace a dependency.
